name: Mahshee
recipeIngredient: 1 cup rice, washed and soaked for an hour
2 tablespoons tomato paste (from a small can)
recipeInstructions:
STUFFED ZUKES (MAHSHEE)
Filling:
---------
1 cup rice, washed and soaked for an hour
2 tablespoons tomato paste (from a small can)
3 tablespoons cooked lentils
1 small onion, grated
3 cloves garlic crushed
1 tablespoon cinnamon
2 teaspoons cumin
2 tablespoons minced fresh cilantro
Salt
Pepper
Fill-ees:
---------
4 zukes with the tops off and the insides scooped out using a coring tool or peeler.
1. Core the zukes and mix the filling ingredients in a bowl.
2. Fill each zuke about 3/4 full with the mixture, securing the top by tapering it with a knife.
3. Place lettuce leaves at the bottom of a large pot (the biggest you have) to prevent burning.
4. Put the zukes bottom-down in the pot, filling any space with additional vegetables or fillings.
5. Cover with water or broth (well-salted and very important).
6. Add the remaining tomato paste from the can, chopped onion, garlic, and cumin.
7. Place a lid on the pot and cook for 45 minutes to 1 hour on medium-high heat until boiling.
8. Check one zuke to ensure the rice is cooked; if it is, remove all the zukes to a serving platter.
9. Spoon some liquid over the zukes (discard lettuce and other ingredients).
10. Optionally, cover with an onion-mint yogurt sauce and garnish with a sprinkle of paprika for color. Enjoy!
